6a00bc922944e5b1dd1cead1.title: Psychological stability: scientific foundations
6a00bc922944e5b1dd1cead1.desc: A detailed guide to developing psychological resilience based on the latest research in the field of neurobiology.
6a00bc922944e5b1dd1cead1.content: Psychological resilience is the ability to adapt to stress and recover from difficulties. Develops through practice.

6a00bc922944e5b1dd1ceacd.title: Breathing for peace
6a00bc922944e5b1dd1ceacd.desc: Master the 4-7-8 technique for instant relaxation and reduced anxiety. This simple but powerful technique helps activate the parasympathetic nervous system.
6a00bc922944e5b1dd1ceacd.content: Breathing technique 4-7-8: Inhale for 4 counts, hold for 7, exhale for 8. Helps to quickly return to a state of rest.

6a00bc922944e5b1dd1ceace.title: Progressive relaxation technique
6a00bc922944e5b1dd1ceace.desc: A step-by-step technique for relieving muscle tension through successive tension and relaxation of various muscle groups.
6a00bc922944e5b1dd1ceace.content: Progressive muscle relaxation: alternately tense and relax muscle groups from the feet to the face.

6a00bc922944e5b1dd1ceacf.title: Mindfulness meditation for beginners
6a00bc922944e5b1dd1ceacf.desc: An introduction to mindfulness practice - focusing on the breath and the present moment without judgment.
6a00bc922944e5b1dd1ceacf.content: Mindfulness Meditation: Sit comfortably, close your eyes, and focus on your breathing. When thoughts distract you, gently bring your attention back to your breath.

6a00bc922944e5b1dd1cead0.title: Diagnostics of the psychological state
6a00bc922944e5b1dd1cead0.desc: Comprehensive assessment of your mental health through analysis of stress, anxiety, apathy and overall life satisfaction.
6a00bc922944e5b1dd1cead0.content: Diagnosis includes assessment of stress, anxiety, apathy and general well-being. Helps identify areas for improvement.

6a00bc922944e5b1dd1cead2.title: Keeping a reflection diary
6a00bc922944e5b1dd1cead2.desc: A step-by-step guide to effective journaling for self-discovery, emotional regulation, and tracking personal growth.
6a00bc922944e5b1dd1cead2.content: A reflection journal helps to develop self-awareness, improve emotional regulation, and find meaning in lived experiences.

6a0388f9db5bdded60e069ec.title: Sleep hygiene under stress
6a0388f9db5bdded60e069ec.desc: How to ensure quality rest when the brain does not want to fall asleep.
6a0388f9db5bdded60e069ec.content:
The importance of sleep during stress

During periods of great stress, our body produces an excess of cortisol, which interferes with falling asleep. Sleep hygiene becomes your main recovery tool.

Basic tips:

  • Schedule: Go to bed and get up at the same time.
  • Digital detox: Put away gadgets 1 hour before bed. Blue light suppresses melatonin.
  • Ritual: Take a warm shower or read a paper book.

6a0388fadb5bdded60e069ed.title: Emotional intelligence
6a0388fadb5bdded60e069ed.desc: Understanding one's own emotions as the key to stability.
6a0388fadb5bdded60e069ed.content:
What is emotional intelligence (EQ)?

EQ is the ability to recognize, understand and manage one's own emotions, as well as to influence the emotions of other people.

Why is this important?

People with high EQ cope better with stress, build stronger relationships and recover faster from crisis situations.

Tip: Whenever you feel a strong emotion, try naming it. "I feel anxious right now" is the first step to control.

6a0388fadb5bdded60e069ee.title: Meditation for beginners
6a0388fadb5bdded60e069ee.desc: Simple steps to inner silence without too much mysticism.
6a0388fadb5bdded60e069ee.content:
Meditation is simple

You don't have to sit in the lotus position for hours. 5-10 minutes a day is enough to start seeing results.

Where to start:

  1. Find a quiet place where you won't be disturbed.
  2. Sit comfortably, keeping your back straight.
  3. Focus on your breathing. Feel the air coming in and out.
  4. When thoughts distract you, just gently return your attention to your breathing.

6a0388fadb5bdded60e069ef.title: How Cortisol Works
6a0388fadb5bdded60e069ef.desc: Understanding the chemistry of stress will help you overcome it.
6a0388fadb5bdded60e069ef.content:
Cortisol: the stress hormone

Cortisol is produced by the adrenal glands in response to a threat. It prepares the body for the "fight or flight" reaction.

Side effects of prolonged stress:

If cortisol levels are consistently high, it leads to exhaustion, digestive problems, and impaired immunity.

How to reduce: Physical activity, laughter and deep breathing help to signal to the brain that the threat has passed.

6a0388fbdb5bdded60e069f1.title: Practice 4-7-8: Breathing technique
6a0388fbdb5bdded60e069f1.desc: An effective way to relax through breath control.
6a0388fbdb5bdded60e069f1.content: This technique helps to quickly calm the nervous system. Use it when you feel suddenly anxious or can't sleep.

6a0388fcdb5bdded60e069f2.title: Yoga for the neck and shoulders
6a0388fcdb5bdded60e069f2.desc: We relieve stiffness and tension after a working day.
6a0388fcdb5bdded60e069f2.content: Most of the stress accumulates in the neck and shoulders. This short complex will help you feel relief.

6a0388fcdb5bdded60e069f3.title: Calm breathing: Meditation
6a0388fcdb5bdded60e069f3.desc: 3 minutes of deep relaxation in Ukrainian.
6a0388fcdb5bdded60e069f3.content: A short but very effective meditation. Just close your eyes and follow the voice.

6a0388fcdb5bdded60e069f5.title: Vagus activation technique
6a0388fcdb5bdded60e069f5.content: Vagus activation is the fastest physiological way to switch the body from fight-or-flight mode to rest-and-digest mode.

6a0388fcdb5bdded60e069f6.title: Active morning: Charging
6a0388fcdb5bdded60e069f6.desc: A simple and effective warm-up for the whole family.
6a0388fcdb5bdded60e069f6.content: Just 5 minutes of active movements in the morning will give you energy for the whole day.

6a0388fcdb5bdded60e069f8.title: The sound of rain
6a0388fcdb5bdded60e069f8.desc: Natural pink noise that creates a cozy acoustic curtain.
6a0388fcdb5bdded60e069f8.content: An hour of soothing rain sounds for sleep or work.

6a1f137c38a0d34868ecfc9b.title: Grounding technique 5-4-3-2-1
6a1f137c38a0d34868ecfc9b.desc: A quick way to come back to reality during times of panic or severe anxiety.
6a1f137c38a0d34868ecfc9b.content:
What is the 5-4-3-2-1 technique?

This exercise helps stop the flow of anxious thoughts by shifting your attention to your senses and your surroundings.

How to perform:

  1. 5 things you can see right now (eg chair, window, tree).
  2. 4 things you can touch (feel the texture of the table, clothes or your hair).
  3. 3 things you hear (car noise, birds singing, refrigerator humming).
  4. 2 things you can smell (the smell of coffee, soap or just fresh air).
  5. 1 thing you can taste (take a sip of water, eat a candy).

Tip: Say it out loud. This creates additional sensory feedback and even better grounding.

6a1f14c10c46c3d837e9d9f1.title: Why we procrastinate and how to negotiate with our brain
6a1f14c10c46c3d837e9d9f1.desc: Easy article about how procrastination is not laziness, but the brain's response to stress. Contains soft techniques to initiate action.
6a1f14c10c46c3d837e9d9f1.content:
Procrastination is not laziness

Often we think we are putting things off because of laziness. But science says otherwise: procrastination is our brain's response to stress or negative emotions associated with a task.

How to deal with it?

  1. The two-minute rule: Agree with yourself to do the task for only 2 minutes. It removes the brain's resistance to a 'big' task.
  2. Forgive yourself: Research shows that people who forgive themselves for procrastinating are less likely to procrastinate in the future.
  3. Divide and conquer: Break a daunting task into micro-steps that don't cause panic.

6a1f14c10c46c3d837e9d9f4.title: What is "digital hygiene" and how to survive in the information noise
6a1f14c10c46c3d837e9d9f4.desc: Practical tips on how to stop checking your phone every 5 minutes and customize the infospace for yourself.
6a1f14c10c46c3d837e9d9f4.content:
Your phone is a tool, not a master

The constant stream of news and notifications exhausts our nervous system. Here's how to take back control:

Practical steps:

  • Black and white screen: Put your phone in black and white mode. The brain loses interest in "gray" icons, and the time spent on social networks decreases.
  • Turn off the unnecessary: Leave notifications only from people. No push from games or stores.
  • An hour without screens: Try to put your phone down an hour before bed. This will significantly improve the quality of rest.

6a1f16100c46c3d837e9db2f.title: What to do during a panic attack
6a1f16100c46c3d837e9db2f.desc: Clear step-by-step instructions for acute anxiety attacks. How to help yourself or loved ones when you feel like you're losing control.
6a1f16100c46c3d837e9db2f.content:
It's scary, but it will pass

A panic attack is your body's false alarm. It reaches its peak in 10 minutes, and then it necessarily declines. You are not going crazy or dying.

First aid:

  1. Breathe into folded hands: This will help balance the levels of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the blood, which will stop dizziness.
  2. Focus on the outside: Find 5 blue objects around you.
  3. Cold water: Wash your face with ice water or hold an ice cube in your hands. Cold activates the parasympathetic nervous system.

6a1f16100c46c3d837e9db31.title: When there is no strength to get out of bed
6a1f16100c46c3d837e9db31.desc: Material for the hardest days. What to do when the body is heavy, and any action seems impossible.
6a1f16100c46c3d837e9db31.content:
Allow yourself to stop

If you can't get up, don't beat yourself up. Your body has completely exhausted its resources. Now your only task is to just be.

The 'Micro-Steps' Strategy:

  • Don't think about the whole day: Think only about the next 5 minutes.
  • The basic minimum: If you don't have the energy to take a shower, just wash your face or wipe it with a wet towel. Can't cook? Eat an apple or a piece of bread.
  • Change position: Try simply moving from bed to the floor or a chair. Changing the physical position sometimes gives an impulse to the brain.

If this condition lasts more than two weeks, be sure to consult a specialist.

6a285e37459593276a50adb2.title: Day forest: crickets and birds
6a285e37459593276a50adb2.desc: The lively atmosphere of the afternoon forest with the chirping of crickets. Perfect for meditation or background work.
6a285e37459593276a50adb2.content: The sounds of the daytime forest help restore concentration and reduce stress levels. Let yourself be transported for a few minutes to a warm summer day.

6a285e37459593276a50adb3.title: Deep sea waves
6a285e37459593276a50adb3.desc: Soothing sound of real sea waves for deep relaxation and stress relief.
6a285e37459593276a50adb3.content: The sound of the sea has a proven therapeutic effect. Rhythmic bursts of waves help to normalize breathing, slow down the heartbeat and prepare for rest.

6a285e37459593276a50adb4.title: Voices of the wild forest
6a285e37459593276a50adb4.desc: Deep immersion in nature: the rich song of forest birds, which relieves tension.
6a285e37459593276a50adb4.content: Birdsong is evolutionarily associated with security. This audio recording will help you feel calm, let go of anxiety and recharge with natural energy.

6a285e37459593276a50adb5.title: Summer rain with a thunderstorm
6a285e37459593276a50adb5.desc: The soothing sound of rain drumming on the roof, with distant rumbles of thunder.
6a285e37459593276a50adb5.content: The sound of rain creates a sense of security and coziness. It's the perfect background for reading, thinking, or getting ready for bed.

6a285e37459593276a50adb6.title: The noise of a campfire in the forest
6a285e37459593276a50adb6.desc: Atmospheric sound of crackling wood and branches in the fire. Warms and soothes.
6a285e37459593276a50adb6.content: Imagine that you are sitting next to a large forest fire. Listen to the cracking of the branches, feel the imaginary warmth of the fire. This is ideal grounding practice.

6a2867f1459593276a50b36a.title: Paradise atmosphere
6a2867f1459593276a50b36a.desc: Soft and relaxing sounds that create a feeling of absolute peace and harmony.
6a2867f1459593276a50b36a.content: This light audio background is specially designed to relieve mental stress. Give your mind a break from the information noise.

6a0c946cdb5bdded60f083d2.name: Resistance to burnout
6a0c946cdb5bdded60f083d2.description: A simulator for identifying signs of professional exhaustion and building healthy boundaries in the work process.

6a0c946cdb5bdded60f083d3.name: Impostor syndrome
6a0c946cdb5bdded60f083d3.description: A trainer for fighting self-doubt before an important presentation.

6a10771a00169fc597155c7a.name: Conflict resolution
6a10771a00169fc597155c7a.description: A simulator for practicing the reaction to complex emotions of the interlocutor. Learn to choose an empathetic path instead of a conflictual one.

6a1f36970c46c3d837e9f7a0.name: Symptoms of burnout
6a1f36970c46c3d837e9f7a0.description: Section burnout symptoms and recovery actions

6a1f36970c46c3d837e9f7a1.name: Thoughts of an impostor
6a1f36970c46c3d837e9f7a1.description: Let's separate harmful thoughts from rational ones

6a1f36970c46c3d837e9f7a2.name: Focus and concentration
6a1f36970c46c3d837e9f7a2.description: Sort out what distracts you and what helps you focus

items.text: A clear plan for the day

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed22951.text: How often have you felt tense in the last week?

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed22952.text: How often do you feel that you are unable to cope with current affairs?

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed22953.text: How hard is it for you to relax after a long day at work?

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed22954.text: How easily do you manage to concentrate?

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed22955.text: How often have you made careless mistakes lately?

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed22956.text: Do you find it difficult to make everyday decisions?

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed22957.text: How would you rate the quality of your sleep?

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed22958.text: How often do you wake up feeling tired?

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed22959.text: Have you noticed a change in your appetite?

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed2295a.text: Do you feel supported by loved ones?

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed2295b.text: How often do you feel anxious for no reason?

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed2295c.text: Are you able to feel joy from ordinary things?

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed2295d.text: How do you feel about your daily responsibilities?

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed2295e.text: Do you feel like your efforts are in vain?

6a038c2689ecfbd18ed2295f.text: How quickly do you recover on the weekend?
options: I do not restore at all

6a1f4e1a0c46c3d837ea0934.title: The two-minute rule during periods of high anxiety
